DISCOVERY 610, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY RESPONSES AND MOTION FOR ORDER ESTABLISHING ADMISSIONS IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF'S FAILURE TO RESPOND WAS A MISUSE OF THE DISCOVERY PROCESS. PLAINTIFF IS ORDERED TO RESPOND WITHIN 15 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. DEFENDANT'S REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION ARE DEEMED ADMITTED. SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED AGAINST PLAINTIFF IN THE AMOUNT OF $800, TO BE PAID WITHIN 30 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT. NOT REPORTED.

MINI MINUTES: THE CARTWRIGHT LAW FIRM'S MOTION TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF ARNULFO GALAN. Appearances: Robert E. Cartwright (433.0444), counsel for plaintiff and moving to be relieved. No other appearances. The Court modified its ruling as follows: denied without prejudice in view of current trial date and sanctions hearing. Counsel made an oral request to vacate the trial date, and was advised to bring the request to the attention of Department 206. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions And Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-23-2010 Continued To Aug-03-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 610
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances, DEFENDANTS KEVIN MCAFEE, DONALD LOUIE and CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion For Terminating Sanctions And Monetary Sanctions, is continued to August 3, 2010 on the Court's own motion. Judge: MONICA A. WILEY, not reported.

MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Jul-02-2010 Continued To Jul-23-10 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
DAVID G. YEN, ESQ. OF THE CARTWRIGHT LAW FIRM, INC.'S MOTION TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL (FOR PLAINTIFF ARNULFO GALAN).

APPEARANCES: Matthew J. Quinlan, Esq. of The Cartwright Law Firm, Inc. 433-0444 moving to be relieved as counsel for pltf; no other appearances.

RULING: Upon request of moving party, the Court continued the matter to 07/23/10 for counsel to renotice/serve defendant. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Court Reporter: Joseph Vickstein, CSR #4780

Defendant City And County Of San Francisco'S Motion To Compel Discovery Responses, For Order Establishing Admissions And For Sanctions (Continued From 6/17/10 And 6/18/10)
Off Calendar Jun-22-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Blake P. Loebs, DCA, for defendant and moving party: City And County Of San Francisco
DAvid G. Yen, Esq., for plaintiff and opposing party: Arnulfo Galan

After hearing, tentative ruling is adopted. Defendant City And County Of San Francisco'S Motion To Compel Discovery Responses and Motion For Order Establishing Admissions is granted. Plaintiff's failure to respond was a misuse of the discovery process. Plaintiff is ordered to respond within 15 days of notice of this order. Defendant's Requests For Admission are deemed admitted. Sanctions are awarded against Plaintiff in the amount of $800, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. Order signed in open court. Commissioner Everett A. Hewlett. Not reported.

Defendant City And County Of San Francisco'S Motion To Compel Discovery Responses, For Order Establishing Admissions And For Sanctions (Continued From 6/17/10)
Off Calendar Jun-18-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances, tentative ruling is adopted. Defendant City And County Of San Francisco'S Motion To Compel Discovery Responses, For Order Establishing Admissions And For Sanctions, is continued to June 22, 2010 at 9:00 a.m. at the request of the moving party. Commissioner Everett A. Hewlett. Not reported.

Judge: Monica Wiley, not reported.
